scopes.docs: Reference documentation, conventions, rules (`.claude/docs/` agent-facing or `docs/` user-facing)

current_goal: **v3 — Final polish before cut.** Addressing important features (memory, context management, bash output compression), architectural decisions (packaging, distribution, sync profiles), and polishing rough edges (test suite performance, frontmatter normalization). P99 tasks are deferred — ignore unless explicitly prompted. The line between "what ships in v3" and "what comes after" is being drawn now.

tasks.title: `surface-docs.sh` hook — context-aware doc surfacing
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P2 2026-04-26 — direct follow-up to `session-start-output-too-large`. The cap fix shrinks `essential-conventions-{code_style,execution}` to Quick Reference (§1) + path nudge; this hook extends the same surfacing pattern to `relevant-*` docs, matching tool context against doc keywords and injecting a one-liner ("relevant doc available: `.claude/docs/<name>.md` — Read on demand") when a relevant doc hasn't been loaded. Reuses the §1-extraction primitive landed in the cap fix. Same deterministic algorithm as `surface-lessons.sh` — intra-session dedup (v2.63.6) + 2+ keyword-hit threshold (v2.63.7). Original P99 demotion rationale ("observe surface-lessons for a few weeks before replicating") is moot: capacity pressure from the 10KB cap forces the issue earlier than the design-validation timeline anticipated, and the cap fix itself validates the §1-surfacing pattern. Coordinates with `.claude/hooks/` queue item 5.

tasks.title: Evaluate independence of lessons ecosystem from analytics ecosystem
tasks.notes:
Should the lessons ecosystem (capture + surface + manage rules) work without the analytics ecosystem (sessions.db, hooks.db, project_paths, indexer)? Today the lessons DB schema is owned by claude-sessions and the projects dimension uses claude-sessions' resolution chain — partial-install users (sessions.db present but stale) hit a strict error. Catalog couplings, decide self-contained mode vs. formalized dependency.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/lessons-analytics-independence.md

tasks.title: Convention refinements from Anbeeld/AGENTS.md patterns
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P3 2026-04-28 — patterns resurfaced during VILA-Lab review. Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration (github.com/Anbeeld/AGENTS.md, 59 stars; github.com/Anbeeld/WRITING.md, 33 stars). Three patterns worth extracting — not adopting their files, just informing our own conventions: (1) **Explicit priority stack** — "correctness > evidence > safety > minimal changes > consistency > performance" as a codified tiebreaker. We have these priorities implicitly but no single ranked list. Could land in `essential-conventions-code_style` or a new lightweight convention doc. (2) **Evidence proportionality** — graduated rule: trivial edit → inspect target file; behavioral/API/dependency change → trace execution path, call sites, constraints, regression surface before editing. Currently lives only in agent-level prompts, not as a global convention. (3) **Regularity tripwire for `write-documentation`** — self-check rule: "name the most repeated pattern; if it appears 3+ times or dominates two consecutive paragraphs, rewrite one." Fits as a required-check in the `write-documentation` skill, not a global convention. Apply when next touching these docs/skills, not as standalone work.

tasks.title: Add structured reasoning activation to select agents
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P3 2026-04-28 — connects to `study-superpowers-optimized-patterns` (P1) which evaluates self-consistency reasoning. Some agents would benefit from explicit reasoning technique activation (CoT, hypothesis-evidence patterns, structured decomposition). `code-debugger` already does this organically with its hypothesis-elimination approach. Audit other agents — candidates: `code-reviewer` (risk assessment reasoning), `goal-verifier` (backward verification logic), `proposal-reviewer` (audience perspective reasoning). Light touch — add reasoning prompts where they'd improve output, not a framework overhaul.

tasks.title: `/design-aws` skill — idea to deployable AWS architecture
tasks.notes: Reference + satellite ready; user-postponed (no dependency blockers). Phased workflow: understand idea → design architecture (output: structured markdown doc) → generate diagram via `/design-diagram` with AWS icons → translate to aws-toolkit input configs (YAML) → review (security-first, then architecture). Leverages aws-toolkit for deterministic generation. Also depends on aws-toolkit v1 input format stability. When skill ships: enforce satellite-contract rule — link out to aws-toolkit docs via CLI convention (see `satellite-cli-docs-convention` task), no duplicated spec in workshop. Design doc: `output/claude-toolkit/design/20260329_1517__brainstorm-idea__design-aws.md`. Drafts: `output/claude-toolkit/drafts/archive/aws-toolkit/` — pre-research on IAM validation tools, cost estimation tools, service selection.

tasks.title: Boundary coverage for `test-suggest-json.sh`
tasks.notes: 5 assertions for a 95-line hook (`suggest-read-json.sh`); missing size-threshold boundary tests (just-under, exactly-at, just-over). Correctness gap (off-by-one in size policy), not a security gap — separated from the P0 settings.json work because it's a different threat class. Add ~3 boundary assertions exercising the threshold value defined in the hook source. Surfaced 2026-04-26 in `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260426_1710__design-tests__expect-test-value-audit.md` (Finding 5).

tasks.title: Re-evaluate `suggestions-box/` as satellite convention + `claude-toolkit send --to`
tasks.notes: Today only the workshop (claude-toolkit) has `suggestions-box/` and a documented triage workflow. Satellites (claude-sessions, aws-toolkit, schema-smith, validation-framework) don't — when sending a note from claude-toolkit *to* claude-sessions on 2026-04-26 (the harness-attachment-types observations), I had to `mkdir -p` the destination ad-hoc. Two related changes to evaluate together: (a) add an optional `--to <project-path>` flag to `claude-toolkit send` so you can write to *another* project's `suggestions-box/` without `cd` — current behavior writes to *this* project's box, which is the receive direction only; (b) standardize `suggestions-box/` (with a tiny CLAUDE.md pointing at the workshop's full triage workflow rather than duplicating it) as part of satellite scaffolding. Deferred to evaluate after the pattern gets organic use — if `--to` lands and gets exercised across satellites, the convention case strengthens; if cross-satellite traffic stays at one note a quarter, the formal convention is overkill. Surfaced 2026-04-26 during the SessionStart cap fix wrap-up.

tasks.title: Remove ecosystems opt-in session-start nudge
tasks.notes: After `ecosystems-opt-in` ships, session-start shows a one-time nudge to projects that predate the new schema (no `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_LESSONS` / `CLAUDE_TOOLKIT_TRACEABILITY` env keys in settings.json). The nudge is self-extinguishing per-project (setup-toolkit writes the keys → nudge stops firing), but the code itself should be deleted once all user projects have been updated. Triggered manually rather than version-based because toolkit ships faster than the user reaches each project. Signal to remove: user says "remove the opt-in nudge" or equivalent. Delete the relevant section from `.claude/hooks/session-start.sh` and any related tests.

tasks.title: Fix `md5sum` for macOS
tasks.notes: macOS ships `md5` (BSD) and `shasum`, not `md5sum`. Hits: `cli/eval/query.sh:44`, `tests/test-evaluation-query.sh:61`. Both suppress errors with `2>/dev/null`, so on macOS every resource gets an empty hash; every resource appears stale on `claude-toolkit eval stale`. Fix: switch to `shasum -a 256` or guard with `command -v md5sum`. Deferred until eval core ships. `mktemp --suffix` portion done in v2.72.10.

tasks.title: Update eval scripts `2>/dev/null` for macOS compatibility
tasks.notes: `cli/eval/query.sh` (lines 44, 117, 131, 162, 230), `tests/test-evaluation-query.sh:61`, and `tests/lib/test-helpers.sh:61` suppress errors with `2>/dev/null` on `md5sum` and `jq` calls. The `md5sum` sites overlap with `macos-md5sum`. The jq sites follow the same dangerous pattern fixed in validators (v2.72.11). Deferred because eval isn't relevant for macOS consumers yet. Analysis: `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260427_1700__analyze-idea__macos-loud-errors.md`.

tasks.title: v3 E3 — `teardown-worktree` artifact-copy scope decision
tasks.notes: Currently copies only `output/claude-toolkit/reviews/*` from worktree to parent at teardown. Does not copy `pr-descriptions/`, `design/`, `plans/`, `sessions/`. Decide: (a) deliberate — keep per-worktree ephemera scoped, only review artifacts persist; or (b) broaden to include other `output/claude-toolkit/` subdirs a user is likely to want after teardown. No clear right answer; needs a decision before implementing.

tasks.title: Add interactive option selection to skills that ask questions
tasks.notes: AskUserQuestion supports single-select, multi-select, and preview panes — but most skills default to open-ended questions. Audit skills that use AskUserQuestion (brainstorm-idea may already use options organically) and convert categorical decision points to structured option selection where it fits. Keep free-text for creative/descriptive input.

tasks.title: Explore resource-aware model routing for agent spawning
tasks.notes: Currently agents hardcode `model: "opus"` or `model: "sonnet"`. Some tasks (simple evaluations, pattern searches, file lookups) could route to Haiku for cost/speed without quality loss. Explore: (1) which agents/tasks are candidates for cheaper models, (2) whether this should be a convention in create-agent or a runtime decision by the spawning skill, (3) what the actual cost/quality tradeoff looks like in practice. Start with a discussion pass, not implementation.

tasks.title: Add `claude-toolkit lessons demote` subcommand
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — workaround (raw SQL one-liner) is documented and the motivating sweep didn't need it. Surfaced 2026-04-26 during the Key-tier crystallization sweep. CLI exposes `promote`/`deactivate`/`absorb`/`crystallize` but no inverse of `promote`. The Key Promotion Contract in `manage-lessons/SKILL.md` §4.5 lists "demote back to recent" as one of three valid paths when revisiting a Key lesson, but the workaround is currently raw SQL: `sqlite3 ~/.claude/lessons.db "UPDATE lessons SET tier='recent' WHERE id='<ID>';"`. Add a thin subcommand mirroring `promote`'s shape (single `--id`, sets tier='recent', no other side effects). Add when convenient. Design context: `output/claude-toolkit/design/20260426_1623__design__key-lessons-review.md`.

tasks.title: Add macOS CI runner
tasks.notes: Demoted P1→P99 2026-04-27 — all code fixes landed and validated by a real macOS consumer; new bash scripts are infrequent enough that CI regression catching doesn't justify the maintenance. GitHub Actions `macos-latest` + `make check`. Revisit if macOS-specific regressions recur.

tasks.title: Per-project customization of detection registry
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — speculative pre-design with no user yet (defer until v1 ships and we have real-world signal on which downstream projects need custom patterns: likely aws-toolkit for AWS shapes, schema-smith for DB shapes). Follow-up to hooks-detection-target-convention. Once the shared detection registry stabilizes, add a layered resolution path so projects can extend or override toolkit defaults via project-local detection-registry.local.json merged at hook-load time. Open design questions (merge strategy, disable-toolkit-entry support, sync-out-via-send, precedence on collisions) deferred until there's a real consumer driving the requirements. Design context: output/claude-toolkit/brainstorm/20260425_1349__brainstorm-feature__hooks-detection-target-convention.md.

tasks.title: Fold `surface-lessons.sh` into `grouped-bash-guard.sh`
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — pure perf (~40ms avg savings) on a baseline that may already be lower after dedup work. Re-measure first before any implementation. `surface-lessons.sh` currently averages ~106ms with ~30-40ms of that being bash+jq startup overhead. Fold the Bash branch into `grouped-bash-guard.sh` to skip a second process spawn; keep Read/Write/Edit path separate or extend `grouped-read-guard.sh` to cover Write|Edit. Constraints: async-injection contract (PreToolUse additionalContext), 5s timeout, current matcher is `Bash|Read|Write|Edit` (wider than grouped-read's `Read`). P2 in `output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260423_2309__analyze-idea__improve-lessons-lifecycle.md`; the 106ms baseline predates dedup + 2-hit threshold and may already be lower.

tasks.title: `review-security` — worthyness diagnostic
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — skill has never been invoked, which is already the answer in spirit. Run invocation-frequency check (same approach as pattern-finder agents diagnostic). Based on data: (a) Keep — content already solid; (b) Sharpen — broaden description triggers and/or add surfacing-hook path; (c) Deprecate — CC's built-in /security-review may cover enough of the surface. Do alongside pattern-finder diagnostic for consistency.

tasks.title: Rename `.claude/docs/` to `.claude/conventions/`
tasks.notes: Demoted P3→P99 2026-04-26 — note's own framing is "schedule when nothing else is touching those paths", which is a P99 timing constraint even though the rename has architectural value. `.claude/docs/` is overloaded — name suggests user-facing docs but contents are agent-loaded conventions/rules (`essential-*`, `relevant-*`, `codebase-explorer/`). Shared name with top-level `docs/` hides the audience split (agent context vs user-facing). "rules" conflates with Claude Code's native rules concept, so `conventions/` is the preferred name. Coordinated rename: (1) move files, (2) update session-start loader and surface-* hooks, (3) update sync paths in CLI + dist profiles (base and raiz MANIFESTs), (4) update CLAUDE.md "Structure" section, (5) update `claude-toolkit docs` command and any skill references (grep for `.claude/docs/`), (6) update downstream satellites' synced copies via next sync. Non-trivial churn.

tasks.title: Evaluate LSP plugin setup for the toolkit ecosystem
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Claude Code has native LSP client support (confirmed in v2.1.121 `--bare` flag docs) with 14 LSP plugins in the official marketplace (`anthropics/claude-plugins-official`). Plugins are thin JSON manifests — e.g. pyright-lsp just declares `{"command": "pyright-langserver", "args": ["--stdio"], "extensionToLanguage": {".py": "python"}}`. Tasks: (1) install `pyright-lsp` plugin, test impact on code navigation speed/accuracy for this bash+python codebase; (2) assess whether custom LSP configs (without the marketplace plugin) are viable — the plugin format is trivial enough to self-author; (3) evaluate what LSP capabilities are actually exposed (blog claims goToDefinition/findReferences/hover — verify against real behavior); (4) decide whether LSP belongs in the toolkit's recommended setup or stays opt-in per-project. Skip Serena (tried before, too much bloat) and typescript-lsp (not needed). Cross-reference with `eval-claude-mem` for broader context-retrieval strategy. Source: karanbansal.in/blog/claude-code-lsp/ (mixed real+fabricated details — use official plugin docs as canonical source).

tasks.title: Validator for ERE-metachar entries in settings.json permissions
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-29 by goal-verifier on hooks-config-driven (review at output/claude-toolkit/reviews/20260429_1217__goal-verifier__feat-hooks-config-driven.md). The lib/settings-permissions.sh loader audits each Bash() prefix for ERE metacharacters and silently skips entries that contain `*?+()[]{}^$\|` (with a stderr warning that no one will see in production). Today's permissions.{allow,ask} are clean, but a future contributor adding e.g. `Bash(npm run|test:*)` or `Bash(node --inspect=*:*)` would see the entry vanish from the hook's effective list with no make-check signal. Add a validate-settings-permissions.sh that runs in make validate: walk both buckets, flag entries that would be silently dropped by the loader. Cheap (single jq + a regex audit). Not a current defect — strictly preventative.

tasks.title: Periodically audit read-only verb allowlist in block-config-edits.sh
tasks.notes: Fix #4 introduced a path-side default-deny for .claude/settings.json: any write-shaped verb alongside the path blocks. Read-only verbs (cat, grep, jq, head, tail, wc, diff, ls, find, stat, file) on the path pass through. As new read-only tools (bat, rg, eza, etc.) become common, false-positive blocks would surface when reading settings — extend the allowlist when that happens. Living as a recurring backlog task because the allowlist isn't enumerated in code (the invariant is documented inline; comment-only persistence won't materialize as a reminder).

tasks.title: Move settings-integrity state file out of repo to ~/.claude/
tasks.notes: The integrity check stores its baseline at .claude/logs/settings-integrity.json — in-repo, gitignored, but LLM-writable. A session can rewrite settings.json then update the state file so the next session sees no drift (silent). Move state to $HOME/.claude/settings-integrity/<project-id>.json (per-machine, outside the repo's hook surface) so tampering requires escaping the project sandbox. Closes the cheapest bypass after committing the rewrite.

tasks.title: Settings-integrity: verify covering-commit author + remote presence
tasks.notes: Current integrity check trusts any commit covering the change as evidence of legitimate edit. An LLM that runs git commit on a settings.json rewrite silences the warning. Tighten by (a) verifying the covering commit's author matches git config user.email/user.name, and (b) checking the commit exists on origin (not just local). Both signals make a programmatic commit harder to forge. Trade-off: noisier on legitimate uncommitted-but-author-matched edits — needs a UX call.

tasks.title: block-config-edits: catch write-through-symlink when match_ doesn't fire
tasks.notes: Fix #4 added a symlink defense (creation + write-through), gated by match_config_edits firing. The match_ predicate fires on settings-shaped paths or a narrow set of write verbs (>, >>, tee, cp, mv) — it does NOT fire on a bare 'echo > /tmp/link' when the link was planted out-of-band. The symlink-creation step is still blocked, so the bypass requires the link to exist before the LLM session — different threat model. Documented gap; revisit if it becomes reachable. Either broaden match_ further (cost: more check_ invocations) or accept and document.

tasks.title: Hooks scope-filter rollout (C7) — implement the path/glob pre-filter the framework designed but didn't ship
tasks.notes: Designed in C7 of the referenced design doc but not implemented (no hook uses it). Defer until hooks-implementation-review (P0) finishes — current perf hotspots are fork/subshell waste, not paths-not-filtered. Re-evaluate after that pass: ship the registry + secrets-guard as a worked example if path pre-filtering still helps, otherwise retire as YAGNI.

tasks.title: Hook validator V12 — generate HOOKS.md from headers, fail on drift
tasks.notes: Add V12 from C4 of the referenced design doc: a render-hooks-index.sh generator that produces docs/indexes/HOOKS.md from CC-HOOK headers, plus a validator check that fails on drift (mirrors the V11 pattern for dispatchers). HOOKS.md is currently hand-maintained — without V12 the SSOT claim is incomplete.

tasks.title: block-credential-exfiltration: explicit test-token allowlist for fixture authoring
tasks.notes: During hook audit, probing block-credential-exfiltration with inline bash commands kept tripping the hook on its own probe payload. Workaround: write fixtures to files via the Edit tool, redirect stdin from the file. Cleaner alternative: add an explicit test-token sentinel (e.g. 'TEST_TOKEN_DO_NOT_FLAG_<hash>') that the hook's regex skips. Tradeoff: any allowlist surface is itself an exfiltration vector if real tokens get prefixed with the sentinel. Probably not worth the complexity unless audit/fixture work scales up — captured as P2 because the workaround is fine for one-off probing. See design/hook-audit/01-standardized/robustness.md.

tasks.title: Investigate 19s per-file gap between predicted parallel wall and measured
tasks.notes: Per-hook-test files measure 5-20s walls but performance.md's per-case fork cost predicts 0.1-0.9s parallel walls. The 5-19s gap per file is unexplained — hypothesis is harness setup overhead in tests/lib/hook-test-setup.sh (mktemp dir creation, env var export, helper sourcing) and xargs -P startup. Worth a separate probe to attribute the gap. If the bulk is per-case I/O (file write + read for batch_run), batching the file writes or using a here-string redirect could compress it. Falls to tests/CLAUDE.md's perf-baseline tracking, not the hook audit. See design/hook-audit/01-standardized/testability.md (Per-hook fork cost).

tasks.title: Explore registry-driven command-shape policy for secrets-guard
tasks.notes: secrets-guard's check-body cost (~26ms p50 per per-hook probe) is dominated by inline command-shape policy (env-listing detection, credential-shaped printenv argument detection). The shared registry today handles regex-based pattern matching; the inline policy is positional ('printenv of a credential-shaped name') and doesn't map cleanly to the current registry shape. Speculative proposal: extend the registry to support positional matching, move secrets-guard's inline policy into registry data, shrink the hook to ~150 LoC. Real performance win (~14ms × all-Bash-firings + all-Read-firings is significant), but it's a substantial change to the registry shape — separate design effort, not a clarity-pass call. P2 because the speculation is real but the win depends on a registry redesign whose cost isn't yet scoped. See design/hook-audit/01-standardized/clarity.md (Proposal 6, secrets-guard subsection).

tasks.title: Per-dispatcher PERF-BUDGET-MS headers (grouped-bash-guard scope_miss=150 scope_hit=220; grouped-read-guard 75/120)
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/02-dispatchers/performance.md. Both dispatchers warn on every make check because they inherit the framework default scope_miss=5. N=30 probe data backs scope_miss=150/scope_hit=220 for grouped-bash-guard and 75/120 for grouped-read-guard (smoke p95 + ~10%). Cheapest fix: one header line per dispatcher. Stops V20 false-positives without hiding +20% drift regressions.

tasks.title: Micro-bench cost of guarded source calls (source X when X is already sourced)
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/02-dispatchers/performance.md cost decomposition. Suspected source of the ~35ms (bash-guard) / ~16ms (read-guard) residual after attributing bash startup, hook-utils parse, hook_init, per-child source, match/check, and substep logging. Hypothesis: each child's source lib/hook-utils.sh + source lib/detection-registry.sh lines hit the idempotency guard (no-op for body), but bash still does command lookup + path resolution per call. With 8 children × ~3 source-lines each ≈ 24 guarded source calls; if each costs ~1-2ms, that closes the residual. Probe shape: bash -c 'source X; for i in {1..N}; do source X; done' bracketed.

tasks.title: Enforce _BLOCK_REASON contract: rc=1 from check_<name> implies _BLOCK_REASON is set
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/02-dispatchers/robustness.md T18. Today's contract is convention-only: a child's check_<name> returning 1 must have set _BLOCK_REASON, but nothing enforces it. A future child writer who forgets the assignment ships a hook that emits decision JSON with empty reason — silent UX defect. Two-part fix: (a) dispatcher-side fallback after the check call (assert _BLOCK_REASON non-empty, otherwise set diagnostic message naming the offending child); (b) validator-side static check (grep for _BLOCK_REASON= near every return 1 in check_<name> functions of dual-mode hooks). Both are cheap. Recorded as P2: T18 reproduces deterministically but no current child violates.

tasks.title: Log skipped(source_failed) substep when dispatcher's source  returns non-zero
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/02-dispatchers/robustness.md T16. Today the dispatcher's generated source-loop runs source  without rc check. A child file whose top-level returns non-zero (dependency error, programmatic abort) is silently skipped: declare -F catches missing functions, but partially-defined functions may register and be invoked on inputs they can't handle. Cheap fix: capture rc of source, emit hook_log_substep check_<name>_source_failed with reason if non-zero, and skip registration even if declare -F passes. Real-world likelihood is low (children define functions atomically) but the contract is brittle.

tasks.title: hook_init: treat empty _HOOK_INIT_TOOL_NAME (rc=0 jq, empty input) as malformed for PreToolUse
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/02-dispatchers/robustness.md empty-stdin edge case. printf '' | jq -r '...' exits rc=0 with empty output, so hook_init's malformed branch (line 267 rc!=0 check) doesn't fire. _HOOK_INIT_TOOL_NAME ends up empty; hook_require_tool returns 0 without setting _HOOK_ACTIVE; PreToolUse safety hooks silent-pass instead of failing closed. Diverges from the documented PreToolUse-fails-closed-on-malformed contract. Lib-level fix in hook-utils.sh: if [ -z  ] under HOOK_EVENT=PreToolUse, hit the malformed branch. No production trigger known (harness always emits valid JSON with tool_name); only probe-surface.

tasks.title: Add block-fall-out fixture per dispatcher (substeps after block are all skipped)
tasks.notes: Today the dispatcher's post-block fall-out loop (grouped-bash-guard.sh:108-114, grouped-read-guard.sh:80-86) has no test coverage. Add a fixture per dispatcher where child #1 blocks (e.g. a catastrophic-pattern command triggers the dangerous child), capture the JSONL row from TEST_INVOCATIONS_JSONL, assert substeps | length matches the dispatcher's child count and all substeps after the block index are skipped. Closes testability gaps #1 and #5. Per-fork cost ~130ms; trivial wall impact. Robustness verified the behavior empirically (T19); this fixture locks it as regression coverage.

tasks.title: Add precedence fixtures for dispatch-order regressions (secrets_guard before config_edits, etc.)
tasks.notes: Today only 2 of test-grouped-bash.sh's 7 cases test precedence (credential_exfil winning over git_safety on a force-push with embedded credential). Other ordering decisions (dangerous before everything; auto_mode before credential_exfil; secrets_guard before config_edits) have no precedence assertions. If dispatch-order.json is reshuffled, these silent-pass through. Add 2-3 fixtures sending a command both children would block on, asserting the block reason matches the earlier child. ~3 cases x ~130ms = ~0.4s wall.

tasks.title: Add _missing_match_check fixture per dispatcher (functions-missing distribution branch)
tasks.notes: The generated dispatcher writes hook_log_substep "check_${name}_missing_match_check" 0 "skipped" 0 when a child file is present but its match_/check_ functions are missing. Verified empirically (robustness T15) but no test asserts it. Stage a temp HOOKS_DIR (raiz-sim machinery from test-grouped-bash.sh shows the pattern), replace one child's body with a file that does NOT define match_<name>/check_<name>, assert the JSONL row contains the _missing_match_check skipped substep. ~2 fixtures, ~30 min each. Lower defensibility than fall-out fixture but locks in a real generator promise.

tasks.title: Document deliberate dispatcher asymmetries (read-guard missing FILE_PATH bail; render file-absent silent)
tasks.notes: Two small comment additions, each ~2 lines: (a) grouped-read-guard.sh — explain why no [ -z "$FILE_PATH" ] && exit 0 like grouped-bash-guard has (because all current Read children handle empty FILE_PATH in their match_ predicates and not_applicable accumulation is the right shape); (b) scripts/hook-framework/render-dispatcher.sh — explain why the file-absent branch ([ -f "$src" ] || continue) doesn't log a substep (file absence is expected for distribution-trimmed deployments like raiz; functions-missing is unexpected and gets a substep row). Locks in deliberate decisions so a future consistency pass doesn't change them without thinking.

tasks.title: Trim dispatcher entrypoint comment bloat (delete Children enumeration, substep-outcome list, ordering paraphrase)
tasks.notes: Audit (2026-05-02) found grouped-bash-guard.sh at 57% comment ratio and grouped-read-guard.sh at 47% — well above the standardized hook band of 25-35%. Three categories of bloat: (1) inline Children enumeration (grouped-bash-guard.sh:16-35 = 20 lines) duplicating dispatch-order.json + each child's CC-HOOK PURPOSE header; (2) substep-outcome enumeration (grouped-bash-guard.sh:41-45) duplicating relevant-toolkit-hooks.md §5; (3) dispatcher-loop ordering paraphrase (grouped-bash-guard.sh:78-81) re-stating #1. Drift already verified empirically: the line 80 paraphrase says 'git_safety next' but git_safety is now position 4 in dispatch-order.json. Action: delete the bloat, replace with one-line pointers to dispatch-order.json + relevant-toolkit-hooks.md §4/§5. Drops grouped-bash-guard.sh from 117 → ~87 LoC; grouped-read-guard.sh from 89 → ~79 LoC. Comment ratio drops into the standardized band. Pure deletion. What stays: CC-HOOK headers, the dispatcher-contract 0/1 return docs, [ -z "$COMMAND" ] reasoning, PERMISSION_MODE parsing rationale, the 'CHECK_SPECS + sourcing loop are generated' pointer.

tasks.title: Add byte-turn cost framing + context-injector failure-mode taxonomy to relevant-toolkit-hooks.md
tasks.notes: From performance.md + clarity.md (finding C1 option 2). Add a Context cost amortization subsection to .claude/docs/relevant-toolkit-hooks.md covering: (1) byte-turn cost model (bytes injected × turns remaining in session) for context-injection hooks vs the per-call cost model for safety dispatchers; (2) context-injector failure-mode taxonomy (total failure / partial payload / stale data / cap exceeded / source unavailable / wrong relevance) — replaces fail-closed/open/soft/loud which doesnt apply to no-decision hooks; (3) implicit policy session-start uses for section ordering (essential foundation → guidance → situational → conditional → lessons → ACK at the end so it survives partial truncation least). Real session-length anchors (claude-toolkit p50=8 turns, p95=33 turns from sessions.db) for the byte-turn arithmetic. Future context-injection hook authors inherit the framing.

tasks.title: Extend validate-session-start-cap.sh to emit per-section bytes (drift visibility)
tasks.notes: From robustness.md + testability.md. Extend .claude/scripts/validate-session-start-cap.sh to emit per-section bytes alongside the total. session-start already emits section banners (=== ESSENTIAL CONTEXT ===, === GIT CONTEXT ===, etc.), so the validator can parse the output. Drift visibility: anyone making session-start grow can see exactly which section is doing the growing. Cross-axis leverage — supports robustness (per-section growth alarm), performance (per-section byte cost data for byte-turn projection), context-pollution (per-doc soft-cap policy enforcement), testability (the breakdown IS regression coverage). Recommended: combine with hook-audit-03-essential-full-inject-discipline into one PR (same file, mutually reinforcing).

tasks.title: Per-doc soft cap for ESSENTIAL_FULL_INJECT members + warning comment at session-start.sh:39
tasks.notes: From context-pollution.md Finding A1 + clarity.md Finding C3. Two parts: (1) per-doc soft cap in validate-session-start-cap.sh — warn if any single full-injected doc (member of ESSENTIAL_FULL_INJECT) exceeds 4000 B; (2) expand the comment at session-start.sh:39-41 with the byte-turn cost framing (e.g. ~3500 B doc costs ~350K byte-turns over 100-turn session). Prevents silent growth of single-source concentration: essential-preferences-communication_style is currently 65% of session-starts payload by byte share. Future maintainers adding a doc to ESSENTIAL_FULL_INJECT need to see the cost. Combine with hook-audit-03-cap-validator-breakdown.

tasks.title: Investigate half-empty active tag vocabulary in lessons.db (populate, deactivate, or document)
tasks.notes: From context-pollution.md B3. Half the active tags in lessons.db have empty `keywords` and silently never satisfy the surface-lessons.sh:116 HAVING(CASE_SUM)>=2 gate. Sample query: `SELECT name, keywords FROM tags WHERE status=active AND (keywords IS NULL OR keywords=)`. Investigate whether these are deliberately disabled (set status=inactive) or accidentally orphaned (populate keywords). If neither, document the convention. Affected tags this session: communication, conventions, docs, resources, scripts, testing.

tasks.title: Add fixtures for the three documented negative cases in session-start.sh:20-34
tasks.notes: From robustness.md Source unavailable. session-start.sh:20-34 documents three negative cases: (1) no docs dir (CLAUDE_DOCS_DIR=/nonexistent → warn + exit 0); (2) empty docs dir (no essential-*.md files → 0 essential docs loaded); (3) no git repo (Branch shows unknown, Main shows main fallback). All three lack regression fixtures under tests/hooks/fixtures/session-start/. Add 3 fixtures + 3 .expect files. Cheap; locks in the documented behavior.

tasks.title: Regression test asserting ESSENTIAL_FULL_INJECT policy (full vs Quick Reference)
tasks.notes: From testability.md Section A Correctness. Test that asserts the ESSENTIAL_FULL_INJECT policy holds: essential-preferences-communication_style content appears in full in session-starts output; other essential-*.md content appears as Quick Reference (§1) + path nudge. Locks in the policy decision from context-pollution.md A1. Today it works but is not regression-protected.

tasks.title: Extend perf-session-start.sh with cold-cache mode + ACTIONABLE_ITEMS scenario flags
tasks.notes: From testability.md Section A Input variance. Extend tests/perf-session-start.sh: -c flag for cold-cache mode (drop file caches between runs OR unique tmpdir per run); -s flag for ACTIONABLE_ITEMS scenarios (toolkit version mismatch, lessons.db missing, opt-in nudge, settings-integrity drift). Today the harness measures only the baseline happy path and would not catch cost regressions on the conditional paths.

tasks.title: Emit per-section bytes-injected from perf-session-start.sh
tasks.notes: From testability.md Section A Token cost. Have tests/perf-session-start.sh emit per-section bytes-injected alongside the timing breakdown. Combined with the byte-turn framing (hook-audit-03-document-byte-turn-framing), gives direct visualization of `section X costs N ms wall-clock and M bytes-per-session of context`. Same data the cap validator emits in aggregate (hook-audit-03-cap-validator-breakdown), just in the perf harness output.

tasks.title: Emit bytes-injected per fire from perf-surface-lessons.sh
tasks.notes: From testability.md Section B Token cost. Have tests/perf-surface-lessons.sh emit bytes-injected per fire (the LESSONS variables length). Combined with hit-rate from surface-lessons-context.jsonl, gives cumulative-bytes-injected projections per session for the byte-turn framing.

tasks.title: Edge-case fixtures: LIMIT 3, scope filter, 3-char filter, JSON-escape (single test file)
tasks.notes: From testability.md Section B Correctness. Single new test file tests/hooks/test-surface-lessons-edge-cases.sh covering four mechanism gaps: (1) LIMIT 3 cap (fixture with 5+ matching lessons asserting only 3 surface); (2) scope filter (l.scope=global vs l.scope=project AND l.project_id=current); (3) 3-character word filter (line 64 `[ ${#word} -lt 3 ] && continue`); (4) JSON-escape edge cases for lesson texts containing control chars (newlines, tabs). Uses stubbed lessons.db like the existing test-surface-lessons-*.sh files.

tasks.title: Audit JSON-escape correctness for lesson texts containing control characters
tasks.notes: From robustness.md Partial payload. The hooks current JSON escape via sed at surface-lessons.sh:145 covers `\` and `"` only. Lesson texts containing literal newlines, tabs, or other JSON-control characters may produce malformed `additionalContext` strings. Audit: does hook_inject (in lib/hook-utils.sh) add escaping on top of the hooks sed? If not, what does the harness do with malformed JSON? Add escape for control chars if needed. Pairs with hook-audit-03-surface-lessons-edge-fixtures (escape fixture is one of the four edge cases).

tasks.title: [idea] Extend perf-surface-lessons.sh with lessons.db-size + tag-density scenarios (defer)
tasks.notes: DEFER. Idea from testability.md Section B Input variance. Extend tests/perf-surface-lessons.sh with lessons.db-size and tag-keyword-density scenarios. Only worth doing if a baseline-comparison need surfaces during eval-claude-mem. Today the synthetic + replay modes are well-shaped for current mechanism.

tasks.title: [watch] Investigate hook_extract_quick_reference truncation behavior if real session produces malformed output
tasks.notes: WATCH-ITEM. From robustness.md Partial payload. hook_extract_quick_reference (used by session-start) may return truncated content if awk script errors partway. Placeholder fallback only fires on empty return, not truncated. Only investigate if a real session ever produces malformed Quick Reference output. No reports today.

tasks.title: [idea] Extend PERF-BUDGET-MS schema with firing_rate=session|per_call annotation (defer until 2nd one-shot hook)
tasks.notes: DEFER. Idea from performance.md Cross-cutting observations. Add `firing_rate=session|per_call|per_event` annotation to PERF-BUDGET-MS header schema. V20 would scale aggregate-budget calculations using the rate. Premature today (only one one-shot hook in the toolkit). Reopen if a second one-shot hook joins the family or if a consumer requests it.

tasks.title: [idea] Track cumulative bytes-injected per session; warn over threshold (out of scope; analytics dependency)
tasks.notes: DEFER (out-of-scope for hook-audit). Idea from performance.md Cross-cutting observations. Track cumulative bytes-injected across all hooks in a session; warn when projected forward exceeds a threshold (e.g. 20 KB across 100 turns). Depends on the analytics ecosystem (sessions.db, hooks.db) for the data, not on hook-framework-internal changes. Reopen if cross-hook saturation surfaces in real sessions.

tasks.title: Move _strip_inert_content from hook-utils.sh to detection-registry.sh
tasks.notes: Design doc Proposal 1 (design/hook-audit/00-shared/clarity.md) claims _strip_inert_content has a single consumer (detection_registry_match_kind). Wrong: enforce-uv-run.sh:28,39 and git-safety.sh:51,64 call it directly while sourcing only hook-utils.sh, not detection-registry.sh. A clean move would break both hooks. Options: (a) drop Proposal 1, (b) migrate the two direct callers to detection_registry_match_kind first, (c) ship a shim. None high-value enough for P0 — bumped to P3 pending a clarity.md revision.

tasks.title: Evaluate centralizing the .example/.template allowlist in secrets-guard
tasks.notes: secrets-guard repeats the credential-safe-suffix regex \\.(example|template)$ in three sites: _match_path_registry env-file branch, check_secrets_guard_grep glob branch, and check_secrets_guard ENV_ALLOW_RE local (applied 4×). Logic is identical; inputs differ (basename vs glob vs verb-regex BASH_REMATCH). A helper _has_credential_safe_suffix(s) would dedupe. .pub allowlist is SSH-key-specific and should stay inline. Cosmetic — no current drift. Score against churn before doing. See .claude/hooks/secrets-guard.sh.

tasks.title: block-config-edits: route Write/Edit branches through a match/check pair
tasks.notes: block-config-edits.sh exposes match_config_edits/check_config_edits but only invokes them in main() for Bash. The Write and Edit branches inline is_blocked_config + is_blocked_settings + _settings_decision (lines ~287-318). For uniformity with the post-cleanup secrets-guard (where all three branches route through their match/check pairs) and so the Shape A test layer covers all paths, refactor: introduce match_config_edits_write/check_config_edits_write (or a single match_config_edits_path/check_config_edits_path covering both Write and Edit), route main() through them, leave the registry-driven detection in place. Surfaced during hook-audit-01 secrets-guard alignment; the audit (clarity.md Proposal 4, inventory.md) classified block-config-edits as dual-mode without flagging the per-branch asymmetry. See design/hook-audit/01-standardized/inventory.md and testability.md (caveats added in branch docs/hook-audit-01-secrets-guard-alignment).

tasks.title: git-safety: route EnterPlanMode branch through a match/check pair
tasks.notes: git-safety.sh exposes match_git_safety/check_git_safety but only invokes them in main() for Bash. The EnterPlanMode branch inlines the protected-branch check (lines ~178-196). Smaller scope than block-config-edits — one extra branch, simpler logic — but the same asymmetry: a Shape A test of the existing pair covers Bash but not EnterPlanMode. Refactor: introduce match_git_safety_planmode/check_git_safety_planmode (or fold protected-branch logic under a unified match/check that branches on $TOOL_NAME), route main() through them. Note: EnterPlanMode forks `git rev-parse` per inventory.md:138, so the predicate split has perf shape implications worth measuring. Surfaced during hook-audit-01 secrets-guard alignment. See design/hook-audit/01-standardized/inventory.md and testability.md (caveats added in branch docs/hook-audit-01-secrets-guard-alignment).

tasks.title: secrets-guard Grep branch: investigate .git/config credential-remote coverage gap
tasks.notes: Read and Bash branches both invoke _git_dir_has_credential_remote against $HOME/.git/config (Read) or $PWD (Bash) when the path/command pattern matches .git/config — blocks only when an embedded user:pass@host credential is present. The Grep branch does not: check_secrets_guard_grep handles path patterns via _path_block_reason and glob patterns inline, but never runs the credential-remote check. Path-shape detection still catches `Grep -e secret .git/config` via the git-config registry entry, but the registry entry has continue (deferred) in _match_path_registry — so .git/config greps fall through and pass. Investigate whether this is intentional (Grep on .git/config is rare, and the wider grep-into-token-strings problem is owned by block-credential-exfiltration) or worth closing for parity. If closing: extend check_secrets_guard_grep with the same _git_dir_has_credential_remote dispatch the other two branches use. Surfaced during hook-audit-01 secrets-guard alignment.

tasks.title: Wire superset-invariant assertion into Shape A test layer
tasks.notes: The superset invariant (check_acts(x) implies match_returns_true(x)) is now documented in .claude/docs/relevant-toolkit-hooks.md §1 (Quick Reference) and §4 (named subsection 'The superset invariant'). It is currently a doc-only convention. Once the Shape A test layer (hook-audit-01-shape-a-match-check-pairs) lands, add an enforcement pass: for each of the 9 dual-mode hooks, generate inputs check_<name> would block and assert match_<name> returns true on each. Per-hook input sources: registry-driven hooks (block-credential-exfiltration, secrets-guard registry branch, block-config-edits) — pull the registry pattern alternation and synthesize matching strings. Inline-policy hooks (block-dangerous-commands, git-safety, enforce-make-commands, enforce-uv-run, suggest-read-json, auto-mode-shared-steps) — curate a small list of 'known acts' inputs per hook. Cost when Shape A exists: ~50 LoC + per-hook input lists. See design/hook-audit/01-standardized/clarity.md Proposal 1.

tasks.title: Add injected-outcome fixture for detect-session-start-truncation
tasks.notes: Smoke runner sandboxes $HOME to a fresh tmpdir, so the transcript file at $HOME/.claude/projects/-<derived>/<session>.jsonl that detect-session-start-truncation reads (line 42-52) doesn't exist — hook always falls through to pass. To pair the outcome, extend tests/hooks/run-smoke.sh with optional <name>.transcript.jsonl sidecar that the runner writes into $tmpdir/fakehome/.claude/projects/-<derived>/<session>.jsonl before invoking the hook. Then add injects-on-truncation-marker fixture. Documented skip in hook-audit-01-paired-outcome-fixtures wrap-up (2026-05-03). Direct consumer of the V20 budget per-outcome split when paired with hook-audit-01-perf-rerun-paired.

tasks.title: match_dangerous misses interleaved-quote evasion (e.g. 'r'm -rf /)
tasks.notes: Distinct from the hook-audit-01-block-dangerous-quote-predicate fix in 2.81.5, which widened the predicate's preceding-character alternation to admit single/double quotes (catches `echo 'rm -rf /'`). This shape interleaves quotes WITHIN the rm token itself: `'r'm -rf /` — bash collapses the three tokens into a single `rm` only after parsing, but match_dangerous works on the literal string and sees no bare `rm`. check_dangerous's normalization pass strips quotes (sed s/["']//g) and would catch it, so this is a textbook superset-invariant violation. Surfaced by the Shape A test layer (tests/hooks/test-match-check-pairs.sh) which carries an xfail probe for this exact input. Once fixed, convert the xfail in the test file to assert_match_hit and update the reference.

tasks.title: Add malformed-stdin fixtures: one per event type (PreToolUse/SessionStart/PostToolUse/PermissionRequest/PermissionDenied/UserPromptSubmit)
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/00-shared/robustness.md and 01-standardized/robustness.md and 02-dispatchers/robustness.md. hook_init's malformed-stdin branch is verified manually across all 13 standardized hooks + 2 dispatchers (fail-closed for PreToolUse safety hooks; fail-open for logging/PermissionRequest/SessionStart) but NO smoke fixture asserts the behavior. Only test-log-tool-uses.sh has an inline malformed-stdin tolerance check (shipped 2.81.5). Add one fixture per event type covering at least one representative hook: PreToolUse(Bash) → block JSON, PreToolUse(Write|Edit) → block, PreToolUse(Grep) → block, PreToolUse(Read) → block, PreToolUse(EnterPlanMode) → block, SessionStart → warn-and-continue (verify ACK still emitted), PostToolUse → silent pass with stdin_raw row, PermissionRequest → fail-open silent, PermissionDenied → silent pass, UserPromptSubmit → silent pass. Locks in the only documented fail-closed behavior the safety hooks have. Cheap (copy/paste from existing fixtures, blank/malform the JSON). Verify behavior matches: bash hook.sh < <(printf 'not-json{').

tasks.title: Shape A lib-level tests: _strip_inert_content, _now_ms, hook_feature_enabled, hook_extract_quick_reference
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/00-shared/testability.md Axis 1. The match/check pairs Shape A layer shipped in 2.81.8 (test-match-check-pairs.sh) covers hook-level dual-mode pairs. This task is the orthogonal lib-level coverage: source the libs in-process, call functions, assert. Functions lacking direct tests today: (1) _strip_inert_content — most complex pure-bash function in the libs (~70 LoC, char-by-char walk), highest value because robustness flagged it as the heuristic-limits surface; ~10 cases pinning heredoc, single-quoted, double-quoted, escaped quote, nested quotes, 8KB heredoc behavior. (2) _now_ms — pin the EPOCHREALTIME-padding fix that prevents the documented 10× small bug; force a 1-digit _frac and assert the result. (3) hook_feature_enabled — three branches (lessons / traceability / unknown). Trivial. (4) hook_extract_quick_reference — file-missing path, file-present-no-block path, file-present-with-block path. Trivial. Estimated ~30 new test cases, ~50 LoC of test code, ~0ms per case at runtime. Could be a single new test-libs.sh file or per-lib (editorial).

tasks.title: surface-lessons: PERF-BUDGET-MS scope_miss=20 scope_hit=60 (per-call path)
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/03-session-context/performance.md. Add `# CC-HOOK: PERF-BUDGET-MS: scope_miss=20, scope_hit=60` header to .claude/hooks/surface-lessons.sh. Numbers backed by perf-surface-lessons.sh n=10. Path decomposition: pass (no SQL match) ~17ms inside-hook, hit (SQL fires + inject) ~47ms inside-hook. Tighter than session-start because surface-lessons is on the per-call hot path (PreToolUse Bash|Read|Write|Edit). The asymmetric scope_miss/scope_hit split (20/60) is meaningful: 67% of invocations take cheap path, 33% take expensive path. Stops V20 false-positive on every dispatch.

tasks.title: Manually label 50 surface-lessons firings for relevance precision
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/03-session-context/testability.md Section B Relevance + context-pollution.md B7. Manually label 50 (context, surfaced_lessons) pairs sampled from ~/.claude/hooks.db.surface_lessons_context (claude-toolkit project, match_count > 0) for relevance: relevant / tangential / not relevant. Compute precision. Direct input to eval-claude-mem (P1) — gives candidate systems (claude-mem, agentmemory) a concrete baseline to compete against. ~25min of manual work for 50 samples; could expand to 200 if first 50 do not paint a clear picture. Output: short writeup in output/claude-toolkit/analysis/ with the precision number + a few representative false-positive examples for the eval task.

tasks.title: Document surface-lessons known limitations in relevant-toolkit-lessons.md
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/03-session-context/context-pollution.md Section B + B7. Add a Known Limitations subsection to .claude/docs/relevant-toolkit-lessons.md documenting: (1) 33% hit rate on PreToolUse(Bash|Read|Write|Edit), 44% on Bash specifically; (2) within-session dedup is in place (surface-lessons.sh:79-102) and bounds intra-session repetition; (3) cross-session recurrence is the visible problem — top 3-lesson tuple touches 261 distinct sessions, ~53% of all claude-toolkit sessions; (4) concept-tag false positives (concept tags like convention/pattern/gotcha reach the 2-hit threshold via coincidental token overlap); (5) cross-project leakage on globals (lessons saved in sibling projects fire in this project because scope=global has no project-relevance gate); (6) half the active tags have empty keywords and silently never match. Sets expectations and motivates eval-claude-mem (P1). Empirical data sourced from ~/.claude/hooks.db.surface_lessons_context (17 880 invocations) and ~/.claude/sessions.db (490 sessions).

tasks.title: session-start: PERF-BUDGET-MS scope_miss=200 scope_hit=300 (one-shot loader)
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/03-session-context/performance.md. Add `# CC-HOOK: PERF-BUDGET-MS: scope_miss=200, scope_hit=300` header to .claude/hooks/session-start.sh. Numbers backed by perf-session-start.sh n=10 (TOTAL=112ms inside hook, WALL=136ms). 200ms scope_miss covers V20 measured 49ms with ~4× headroom; 300ms scope_hit covers conditional growth paths (lessons block, toolkit version mismatch, ACTIONABLE_ITEMS items). Add 1-2 line comment alongside header explaining `firing_rate=session` rationale. Stops the recurring V20 false-positive on every make check. Pattern matches 02-dispatchers/clarity.md Proposal 1 (already on backlog as hook-audit-02-perf-budget-headers).

tasks.title: Bash output compression hook — token savings via smart output filtering
tasks.notes: PreToolUse(Bash) hook that intercepts Bash calls, classifies via regex, compresses output before it enters context. Two tiers (near-lossless: install/git verbs → one-line summaries; smart filtering: status/log/test/build/lint/ls truncations). Safety rails: never compress diffs, file reads, piped commands, --verbose/--debug, curl/wget, failed commands, or output <200 chars. Adaptive re-run: same command within 60s passes raw. Fail-open on any hook error. Source (REPOZY/superpowers-optimized) claims ~76% token savings. Tasks: feasibility (bash vs Node, interaction with grouped-bash-guard), tier scope, prototype + measure. Surfaced 2026-04-28; exploration entry: output/claude-toolkit/exploration/BACKLOG.md.

tasks.title: Suggestion hook for piped/repeated make check (catch the discipline drift)
tasks.notes:
Empirical: 36% of make check invocations are piped (205/574 in claude-toolkit hooks.db). Memory + CLAUDE.md doc-only enforcement is insufficient — agents drift even after correction. Add a PreToolUse(Bash) hook that surfaces (not blocks) when make check is filtered or re-run quickly after a fail. Likely folds into grouped-bash-guard.sh or enforce-make-commands.sh.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/make-check-discipline-hook.md

tasks.title: Hook: block destructive SQL statements via Bash
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-30 during hook-framework-refactor design review — guardrail gap. block-dangerous-commands covers filesystem catastrophes but nothing covers irreversible SQL run via Bash (sqlite3, psql, mysql, duckdb invocations, plus interpreter-body writes via python -c). Pattern: mirror block-dangerous-commands shape — match_ on token presence, check_ on structural conditions (e.g. unconditional row deletion, schema-object teardown). Use _strip_inert_content to avoid matching SQL inside comments / heredocs / string literals. Allowlist for test fixtures via env or per-project settings. Lives at .claude/hooks/block-destructive-sql.sh, dispatched by grouped-bash-guard. Land after hook-framework-refactor so the new hook gets the header grammar + smoke test from the start. Reference test shape: tests/hooks/test-block-dangerous-commands.sh.

tasks.title: Buffer dispatcher substep rows; flush all rows in one jq invocation at EXIT trap
tasks.notes: From design/hook-audit/02-dispatchers/performance.md and 02-dispatchers/clarity.md Proposal 6. grouped-bash-guard real-mode pays 8x hook_log_substep jq forks per dispatch (~40ms). Buffer (name, duration_ms, outcome, bytes) tuples in bash arrays during the dispatcher loop; emit ALL rows via one jq -c -n --argjson rows [...] invocation at the EXIT trap. Same N rows in JSONL output (schema preserved); one fork instead of N. Save: ~35ms on bash-guard real-mode, ~10ms on read-guard real-mode. Implementation: four bash array globals initialized at hook_init, dispatcher loop appends instead of direct hook_log_substep call, post-block skipped emission also buffers, EXIT-trap path calls a new flush function. EXPLICITLY REJECTED variants: collapsing N substep rows into one dispatch row with nested substeps array (would break downstream JSONL consumers); skipping jq for substep rows by hand-rolling JSON via printf (fragile against schema additions in hook-logging.sh). Re-run run-per-dispatcher-probe.sh after change for before/after numbers.

tasks.title: Declarative sync profiles — projects choose which resource categories to receive
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Base dist currently syncs ~34 skills to every project regardless of relevance — schema-smith gets `design-docker`, `design-aws`, `brainstorm-idea`, etc. Each unused skill still appears in the session-start skills list, consuming context tokens for zero value. VILA-Lab's "context as scarce resource" principle and Crosley's migration story (400 lines of always-loaded rules → contextual skills → 40% context reduction) both validate: don't ship what won't be used. Proposal: projects declare what they want via a manifest file (e.g. `.claude/sync-profile.json` or similar) listing resource categories or individual resources to include/exclude. `claude-toolkit sync` reads the profile and only copies matching resources. Default behavior (no profile file) = current behavior (sync everything). Categories could be coarse (`skills`, `hooks`, `agents`, `docs`) or domain-based (`core`, `frontend`, `aws`, `database`, `workflow`). Start with a design pass to decide granularity — category-based is simpler but domain-based maps better to actual usage patterns. Related to but distinct from `eval-cli-raiz-dist` (which is about whether the CLI itself ships to raiz, not about what resources ship to base).

tasks.title: Evaluate syncing the CLI to raiz dist
tasks.notes: Evaluate whether and how to include `claude-toolkit` CLI in the raiz distribution profile. Open questions: (1) which subcommands make sense downstream — full CLI or a reduced subset (e.g. `backlog`, `docs`, `lessons search`, `validate` but not `sync`, `eval`, `send`); (2) install story — pip dependency, vendored script, or something else; (3) README / getting-started updates for raiz consumers who'd now have CLI access; (4) whether the CLI needs a consumer-vs-workshop mode flag or if subsetting at dist time is enough. Also consider the manifest model difference: base uses exclude-based (everything ships unless excluded), raiz uses explicit (only declared resources ship). CLI inclusion means declaring it explicitly in the raiz MANIFEST — decide granularity (whole `cli/` tree vs cherry-picked subcommands) and whether the explicit manifest model naturally handles the subsetting or needs a new pattern. Output: analysis doc with recommendation, then implementation if greenlit.

tasks.title: Rethink docs as a resource — absorb into rules and knowledge skills
tasks.notes: Instead of migrating DOCS.md to JSON-backed render (deferred from feat/json-backed-indexes), step back: docs may be the wrong primitive. Claude Code already has 'rules' as a first-class concept, and knowledge-only skills (e.g. read-json) already cover the 'load on demand' shape. Investigate: which .claude/docs/* belong as rules (always-on conventions), which as knowledge skills (load-on-demand reference), which to remove. Goal is to stop swimming against the current — fewer custom primitives, more leverage of native ones. Reference: .claude/docs/ contents, docs/indexes/DOCS.md, list-docs skill.

tasks.title: Move workshop-authored resources out of .claude/ into a dedicated source dir
tasks.notes:
.claude/ in this repo plays two roles: workshop-authored output (synced to consumers) and the toolkit's own dogfooded Claude Code config. Proposal: move workshop sources into a dedicated dir (resources/) so .claude/ recovers its consumer-project meaning. Dogfood via an explicit sync --self step, not symlinks. Land after the hook-audit design phase. Run /shape-project before any code moves.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/workshop-resources-out-of-dotclaude.md

tasks.title: Add prose cleanup resource to `write-documentation` + fix `draft-pr` verbosity
tasks.notes: Surfaced 2026-04-28 during web ecosystem exploration. Two parts: (1) **write-documentation prose resource** — add a kill/replace reference file (based on Clairvoyance humanizer patterns) covering: significance inflation ("powerful" → what it does), sycophantic openers (delete, start with answer), hedge stacking (one qualifier max), empty transitions (delete "Furthermore"/"Moreover"), list addiction (only for sequential steps or equal-weight items), corporate buzzwords ("leverage"→"use"), em-dash overuse (one per paragraph max, zero better), filler phrases ("in order to"→"to"), passive voice (use only when actor unknown), enthusiasm inflation (tone matches content). Concrete before/after examples for docs, commits, PR descriptions. Fits as a `resources/` file in the skill, not inline in SKILL.md. Connects to `convention-refinements-anbeeld` regularity tripwire. (2) **draft-pr vibe fix** — current output is off-vibe: sections feel over-constructed and verbose. Audit the skill's PR template and tone instructions, tighten to match the prose cleanup resource. The commit/PR examples from the humanizer are directly applicable: "Refactor auth: extract token validation" not "This PR introduces a comprehensive suite of improvements."

tasks.title: Frontmatter normalization + token size validator
tasks.notes: Promoted P99→P2 2026-04-28 — token size validator adds concrete value beyond polish. Two parts: (1) **Frontmatter field ordering** — `build-communication-style` uses non-standard order (`name, description, argument-hint, allowed-tools, type`); most skills use `name, type, description, ...`. The A1 sweep resolves `type:` placement but doesn't normalize broader ordering. Could be automated with a small linter or sed pass. (2) **Token size validator** — validation script that flags oversized resources (skills, agents) that get listed in the system prompt. The Agent Skills spec (agentskills.io) recommends <5000 tokens for SKILL.md body; name+description (~100 tokens) loads at startup via progressive disclosure. A script that measures and warns on bloat would catch it before shipping. Integrate into `make validate` or as a standalone `claude-toolkit validate` check.

tasks.title: Revisit toolkit documentation for consumers — CLI help, guided introduction
tasks.notes:
Downstream users landing on a freshly-synced project have no clear entry point. Two gaps: (1) CLI help quality — examples per subcommand, organized top-level help; (2) guided introduction — possibly a /welcome-toolkit or /tour-toolkit skill walking through what got synced and how to use it. Audience is a Claude Code user opening a project that already has the toolkit synced, not someone setting up the workshop.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/docs-consumer-experience.md

tasks.title: General revision of test suite — consolidate patterns, centralize definitions, mark special cases explicitly
tasks.notes:
Pass over the entire bash test suite (tests/test-*.sh, tests/hooks/test-*.sh, tests/lib/) and bring it to one coherent shape. Consolidate repeated assertion/setup helpers, centralize fixtures/expectations, mark special cases with structured comments, standardize file shape, document the result in tests/CLAUDE.md. Don't break the 45-50s wall-clock baseline; flake-fixing is owned by test-runner-reliability.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/test-suite-consolidation.md

tasks.title: Redesign lessons ecosystem (curation lifecycle, storage, surfaces)
tasks.notes:
Scrap-and-redesign the lessons ecosystem (curation lifecycle, storage, surfaces). Independent of LSP pilot — they can run in parallel. Calibrate the eval-claude-mem framing first (analysis miscounts absorbed/crystallized as inactive). Next step: /brainstorm-feature.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/lessons-redesign.md

tasks.title: Survey: how would an agent bypass hook enforcement?
tasks.source: 2026-05-06 grounding session — surfaced while triaging secrets-guard-shell-history-coverage
tasks.notes:
Adversarial survey of the hook layer. Wear an attacker hat, enumerate the bypass surface across reader substitution, path-shape evasion, indirection, tool-channel switching, output side channels, hook-disabling vectors, TOCTOU. Deliverable is a writeup at output/claude-toolkit/analysis/<date>__hook-bypass-survey.md with bypass categories, examples, and triage. Concrete close-the-gap items spawn their own tasks. Defense-in-depth, not blocking — P3 reflects 'do when there's slack'.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/hook-bypass-adversarial-survey.md

tasks.title: Normalize EnterPlanMode plan filenames via PostToolUse(Write) hook
tasks.notes:
PostToolUse(ExitPlanMode) hook that writes a normalized sibling next to EnterPlanMode's whimsy-named plan file. Whimsy file stays as Claude's edit locus; normalized copy is searchable. Single-fire on plan confirmation — payload contains everything the hook needs (planFilePath + plan text). Depends on timestamp-standard-define-and-backfill for the filename format.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/plan-name-normalization-hook.md

tasks.title: Enforce backlog notes length limit (externalize to backlog-notes/)
tasks.source: 2026-05-06 grounding session — after externalizing 8 over-stuffed task notes
tasks.notes:
claude-toolkit backlog validate should fail when a task's notes exceeds the threshold (proposed: 800 chars). Long content lives in output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/<id>.md; in-backlog note becomes a slim pitch + pointer. Make threshold configurable. Sweep existing backlog before flipping the validator on so make check doesn't immediately fail.

See: output/claude-toolkit/backlog-notes/backlog-notes-length-validator.md

tasks.title: Add early-phase parallel-hypothesis step to code-debugger
tasks.notes: Pre-loop step: generate 3 independent root-cause hypotheses from symptoms alone (no shared framing), check clustering, stop and report ambiguity if they scatter. Then enter the existing hypothesis-elimination loop. Targets the anchoring failure mode where hypothesis 2 inherits framing from hypothesis 1. Source: study-superpowers-optimized-patterns.md (Pattern 1 verdict). Self-consistency reasoning, Wang et al. ICLR 2023.

tasks.title: Add rule-restatement check to evaluate-* rubrics
tasks.notes: Add a sub-check (under D5 Progressive Disclosure or as a new dimension) that penalizes the same rule expressed in multiple scoring tables or restated across sections within a single file. Cost: reconciliation overhead at runtime. Apply to evaluate-skill, evaluate-agent, evaluate-docs, evaluate-hook. Source: study-superpowers-optimized-patterns.md (Pattern 2). AGENTbench instruction-volume finding.

tasks.title: Trim D1/D2 restatement in evaluate-skill rubric
tasks.notes: evaluate-skill/SKILL.md describes D1 in three different scoring tables (Dimension Adjustments, D1 Scoring Calibration for Command Type, Scoring Calibration -> D1). D2 has similar partial restatement. Consolidate into one calibration per dimension with a clear branch for command vs knowledge type. Apply the heuristic from evaluate-rubrics-rule-restatement-check to its own author. Source: study-superpowers-optimized-patterns.md (Pattern 2).

tasks.title: Anti-pattern note in create-skill: don't recap own turns
tasks.notes: Add to create-skill/SKILL.md anti-patterns: 'Don't ask the agent to summarize its own prior turns into a new prompt; persist durable state to a file instead.' Smell to flag in skill review. Source: study-superpowers-optimized-patterns.md (Pattern 3). 'Do LLMs Benefit from Their Own Words?' — models over-condition on their own previous outputs.

tasks.title: Investigate transient flake in test-verify-external-deps under make check
tasks.notes: During the 2.83.0 wrap-up make check run, test-verify-external-deps failed once on the "Expected ✓ bash in output" assertion even though the captured output did contain `✓ bash`. Re-running the test in isolation passed 19/19; the next make check passed cleanly. Not reproduced. Likely a parallel-runner quirk (TEMP_DIR, fixture race, or stdout/stderr interleaving under load) but unverified — investigate if it recurs. Source: 2026-05-06 timestamp-standard-iso-t branch wrap-up.

tasks.title: Hook: cover stdin/file-driven SQL gap in block-destructive-sql
tasks.notes: Follow-up to hooks-block-destructive-sql (depends-on). The base hook documents stdin/file-driven SQL as out of scope: 'psql < destructive.sql', 'cat script.sql | sqlite3 db', heredoc bodies (blanked by _strip_inert_content before structural matching). These vectors carry the same blast radius as the inline -c/-e form the base hook covers. Options to evaluate: (1) detect the redirection/pipe shape and read the file from disk for a structural check — cheap, but misses dynamic content; (2) detect heredoc opener targeting an SQL CLI before _strip_inert_content runs and inspect the body; (3) accept the gap permanently and add only the heredoc-to-SQL-CLI case (most likely vector from an in-conversation paste). Land after base hook ships and 1-2 weeks of telemetry shows whether real false negatives are common enough to justify the extra surface. Refs: output/claude-toolkit/analysis/20260506T1407__analyze-idea__hooks-block-destructive-sql.md §3 'Resolution' and §Risks; .claude/hooks/lib/hook-utils.sh:107 (_strip_inert_content).

tasks.title: V20 perf budget conflates predicate-miss with predicate-hit-pass
tasks.notes: scripts/hook-framework/validate.sh check_V19_V20 picks 'scope_miss' when outcome=pass and 'scope_hit' when outcome=block — but a hook whose match_ predicate fires AND whose check_ then passes still has outcome=pass, so it gets measured against scope_miss despite running the full check body. block-destructive-sql exposes this: its allow-predicated-delete fixture (predicate hits on DELETE+psql, check runs paren-aware scan, then passes) lands in scope_miss=45 budget but actually does ~50ms of real work, indistinguishable from a true predicate-miss. Honest fix needs a third bucket — scope_miss (predicate didn't fire), scope_hit_pass (check ran and passed), scope_hit_block (check ran and blocked). Or expose the predicate outcome via hook_log_substep and key the budget on that. Surfaced by code-reviewer agent + user during hooks-block-destructive-sql implementation.

tasks.title: block-destructive-sql: handle bash ANSI-C $'...' quoting
tasks.notes: _extract_quoted_after treats single-quote as a literal-quote opener and doesn't recognize the bash ANSI-C $'...' form. ANSI-C bodies use C-style escapes that EXPAND into real characters before the SQL CLI sees them, so destructive content can hide behind \n / \t / \x escapes. Fix options: (a) detect leading $ before the opening quote, strip it, then process backslash escapes when emitting the body; (b) document $'...' explicitly as out-of-scope and align with hooks-block-destructive-sql-stdin-coverage. Surfaced by /review-security MEDIUM #4 during hooks-block-destructive-sql implementation. Probe artifacts at output/claude-toolkit/sec-probes/ (probes2.txt A22/A27 cases).

tasks.title: Sweep blocking hooks for 'local x=$1 len=${#x}' silent-noop anti-pattern
tasks.notes: Hit twice during block-destructive-sql implementation: 'local in=$1 out= len=${#in}' on a single line evaluates ${#in} before in is assigned in some bash configurations, leaving len=0 — the function silently does nothing. For pure-validation helpers this presents as 'function returns false / never matches', which in a blocking guardrail looks identical to 'no destructive content found' and the hook silently allows. Sweep all PreToolUse blocking hooks in .claude/hooks/ for this pattern (single-line locals where one assignment derives from another via parameter expansion) and split them into separate local statements. Also worth adding a one-paragraph note to .claude/docs/relevant-toolkit-hooks.md so the next contributor doesn't relearn it. Surfaced by /review-security INFO #6.
